purpose: Templates and canonical test vectors for NAS Controller binary protocol verification

schema_version.description: Version of this JSON schema (used to detect schema changes)

vector_version.description: Version of this specific test vector

description.description: Detailed description of what this vector tests

protocol_version.description: Protocol version this vector applies to

firmware_version.description: Firmware version that generated this vector (null until verified)

firmware_commit.description: Firmware git commit that generated this vector (null until verified)

packet_before_crc.description: Input bytes to CRC algorithm (the data that gets checksummed)

fields.crc_value: The computed u16 value (null until verified)
fields.transmitted_crc_bytes: Array of CRC bytes as transmitted [MSB, LSB] (null until verified)

expected_complete_packet.description: Complete packet including CRC and footer (null until verified)

verification_workflow.step_1: Create vector TEMPLATE with input bytes but null CRC values
verification_workflow.step_2: Execute firmware reference implementation on packet_before_crc bytes
verification_workflow.step_3: Capture actual CRC output from firmware execution
verification_workflow.step_4: Transmit complete packet and capture transmitted bytes
verification_workflow.step_5: Record actual values in crc_output and expected_complete_packet
verification_workflow.step_6: Mark verification.status as 'verified' and record date/who
verification_workflow.step_7: Canonical vector is now FROZEN and usable by all implementations

vector_statuses.pending_firmware_execution: TEMPLATE: Input bytes specified, CRC values are NULL. Awaiting firmware to generate values.
vector_statuses.verified: CANONICAL: Firmware has executed and generated values. CRC and packet bytes are FROZEN. Ready for use.
vector_statuses.deprecated: OBSOLETE: This vector has been superseded. Implementations may still use for backward compatibility, but new implementations should prefer replacement vector.

critical_rules.rule_1: NEVER use fabricated CRC values. Only firmware-generated values are valid.
critical_rules.rule_2: NEVER use calculated CRC values. Only executed firmware output is valid.
critical_rules.rule_3: NEVER guess packet bytes. Only captured transmitted bytes are valid.
critical_rules.rule_4: Use JSON null for unknown values, never strings like '[PENDING]'.
critical_rules.rule_5: Each vector must specify exact firmware version and commit that generated it.
critical_rules.rule_6: Canonical vectors are immutable once verified (create new vector for changes).
critical_rules.rule_7: All implementations must produce identical bytes (not just identical CRC values).

interoperability.requirement: Every implementation must produce identical packet bytes for all canonical (verified) vectors.
interoperability.enforcement: Automated test harness compares outputs byte-for-byte during CI/CD

steps: Execute firmware test harness against each template
Record actual CRC outputs from firmware
Update vector JSON files with verified values

notes: This metadata describes TEMPLATES (pending verification), not canonical vectors yet. Templates become canonical only after firmware execution confirms values. See docs/PROJECT_RULES.md Rule 14 for canonical vector definition.
